intended_use: The SKOUTTM system is a software device designed to detect potential colorectal polyps in real time during colonoscopy examinations. It is indicated as a computer-aided detection tool providing colore
intended_use_full: The SKOUTTM system is a software device designed to detect potential colorectal polyps in real time during colonoscopy examinations. It is indicated as a computer-aided detection tool providing colorectal polyps location information to assist qualified and trained gastroenterologists in identifying potential colorectal polyps during colonoscopy examinations in adult patients undergoing colorectal cancer screening or surveillance.
target_condition: colorectal polyps during screening or surveillance colonoscopy
ground_truth: Pathology findings from resected polyps; expert gastroenterologist review and validation of annotations
other_metrics: Object Level True Positive Rate (TPR): 97.87% (95% CI: 94.96, 100.0); Object Level False Positive (FP): 22.55 per 15-minute interval (95% CI: 18.954, 26.148); Frame level TPR: 55.66% mean (95% CI: 55.50, 55.83); Frame level FPR: 2.31% mean (95% CI: 2.16, 2.44); Adenomas per colonoscopy (APC) - statistically significant increase in treatment arm vs control
